intitule: Reading Comprehension: Local Customs and Cultural Practices

enonce.texte:
Read the folktale below and answer the questions that follow.

The Tortoise and the Birds

Long ago, there was a drought in the village. The birds decided to fly to the sky to ask for rain. The tortoise wanted to go with them, but he had no wings. The birds gave him feathers, and he flew with them. In the sky, the tortoise danced and sang. He forgot to thank the birds for their help. The birds were angry. They took back their feathers, and the tortoise fell back to the ground. Since that day, the tortoise has always walked slowly and carried his shell on his back. This folktale teaches us to be grateful and humble.
images: An illustration of a tortoise with feathers flying in the sky with birds, and another scene showing the tortoise falling back to the ground with a sad expression.

questions.texte: Identify what the birds decided to do during the drought.
questions.reponse: The birds decided to fly to the sky to ask for rain.

questions.texte: Explain why the tortoise wanted to go with the birds.
questions.reponse: The tortoise wanted to go with the birds because he also wanted to go to the sky, but he didn't have wings to fly.

questions.texte: Justify why the birds became angry with the tortoise.
questions.reponse: The birds became angry with the tortoise because he forgot to thank them for giving him feathers and helping him fly to the sky.

questions.texte: Analyse the moral lesson of this folktale.
questions.reponse: The moral lesson of this folktale is that we should always be grateful and humble. The tortoise's forgetfulness and pride led to his downfall. It teaches us to appreciate the help others give us and to remain humble even when we achieve success.

enonce.texte:
Listen to the description of a traditional wedding ceremony and complete the sentences below.

(Imagine you are listening to a story about a traditional wedding in Cameroon.)

The traditional wedding ceremony in Cameroon is called "The Dowry Ceremony". The groom's family brings gifts to the bride's family. They bring food, drinks, and money. The bride's family welcomes them with dances and songs. The couple exchanges vows and rings. Everyone celebrates with feasting and dancing.
images: An illustration of a traditional wedding ceremony in Cameroon with people dancing in colorful traditional attire, with gifts and food displayed.

questions.texte: What is the traditional wedding ceremony in Cameroon called?
List the gifts that the groom's family brings to the bride's family.
questions.reponse: The groom's family brings food, drinks, and money.

questions.texte: Describe how the bride's family welcomes the groom's family.
questions.reponse: The bride's family welcomes the groom's family with dances and songs.

questions.texte: Explain what happens after the couple exchanges vows and rings.
questions.reponse: After the couple exchanges vows and rings, everyone celebrates with feasting and dancing.

enonce.texte: Use pre-modifiers (common adjectives) to modify the nouns in the sentences below. Choose from the list: traditional, cultural, beautiful, colourful, old, local, important, special.

questions.texte: We celebrated a __________ festival in our village.
questions.reponse: We celebrated a traditional festival in our village.

questions.texte: My grandmother told me an __________ folktale.
questions.reponse: My grandmother told me an old folktale.
The cultural ceremony was very interesting.
We should preserve our local customs.

intitule: Writing: A Short Story about Local Customs

enonce.texte:
Write a short story (about 80-100 words) about a traditional practice in your community. It could be about a wedding, a funeral, a festival, or any other cultural event.

Your story should include:

  • A clear introduction of the event
  • A description of what happens
  • A conclusion about the importance of the tradition

Use at least three pre-modifiers (adjectives) in your story.
images: A general illustration of a traditional celebration in a village with people dancing, playing drums, and wearing traditional attire.

questions.texte: Write your short story about a traditional practice in your community.
questions.reponse: Last year, I attended a traditional wedding in my village. The ceremony was very colourful. The bride wore a beautiful white dress with traditional beads. The groom and his family brought many gifts. They brought food, drinks, and money for the bride's family. There was a lot of dancing and singing. Everyone was happy and celebrated together. This special tradition is very important in our culture because it brings families together. I will never forget this wonderful celebration.

enonce.texte:
Write a descriptive paragraph (about 60-80 words) about a local cultural practice in your community. It could be about traditional dances, food, attire, or any other custom.

Use at least three pre-modifiers (adjectives) in your description.

questions.texte: Write your descriptive paragraph about a local cultural practice.
questions.reponse: In my community, we celebrate the annual harvest festival. This colourful event brings all the villagers together. The people wear traditional clothes and perform lively dances. The women cook delicious food using local ingredients. The children play drums and sing songs. This important festival reminds us of our ancestors and their hard work. We are proud of our cultural heritage.

enonce.texte:
Write a short dialogue (at least 8 exchanges) between two friends. One friend is from a different culture and wants to know about local customs in Cameroon. The other friend explains some of the customs.

Include at least three pre-modifiers (adjectives) in the dialogue.

questions.reponse:
Marie: Hello! I'm new here. Can you tell me about some local customs in Cameroon?
John: Of course! We have many interesting customs. One special tradition is the dowry ceremony.
Marie: What happens during that ceremony?
John: The groom's family brings gifts to the bride's family. They bring food, drinks, and money.
Marie: That sounds wonderful! What else?
John: We also have colourful festivals where people wear traditional clothes and dance.
Marie: I would love to see that! Do you have any other customs?
John: Yes, we respect our elders very much. This important value is part of our culture.
Marie: Thank you for telling me. I'm excited to learn more about your culture!
